what_it_does: Enumerates every event type a Claude Code session can emit and binds each to a render contract: where it appears, which UI component draws it, how raw fields map to UI fields, and behavioral flags such as terminal, blocking, update target, and stream accumulator.
observations: The file is versioned (schema_ver: 1) and declares itself as the single registry of truth for render contracts.
It defines closed vocabularies for surfaces, placements, components, update targets, stream accumulators, and evidence sources.
Each declaration uses a consistent shape: surface, placement, component, is_terminal, blocks_execution, evidence, optional update_target/stream_accumulator, field_map, and summary.
Lookup law is explicit: exact type/subtype, then family type/*, then bare type, then UNDECLARED.
Sub-dispatch rules cover assistant/user content blocks, stream_event inner events, and attachment types.
Family-fallback entries render unknown subtypes visibly and record a registry-gap drop.
Evidence is tagged per entry as observed-stream, observed-transcript, atlas-docs, inferred, family-fallback, or supervisor-native.
Supervisor wire events (init, text, tool, injected, interrupt_sent, done, closed, keepalive, declared) are included as a separate surface.
Some entries are marked terminal (result/success, result/*, supervisor/closed) and some block execution (AskUserQuestion, auth_status).
field_map values are dotted paths into the raw event object.
standouts: Registry-is-truth design: no ad-hoc JSON; every emitted event must have a declared contract or fall through a loud fallback.
Family-fallback / gap-pressure law: unlisted events still render and are logged as registry pressure rather than silently dropped.
Hierarchical sub-dispatch for content blocks, stream events, and attachments.
Inclusion of supervisor-native wire shapes alongside stream and transcript events.
conventions: Closed vocabularies enumerated under the vocab object.
field_map maps output field names to dotted source paths.
Evidence tags are drawn from a fixed vocabulary.
Consistent behavioral flags: is_terminal, blocks_execution, update_target, stream_accumulator.
concerns: system/session_state_changed is explicitly marked inferred and unverified.
assistant.content.redacted_thinking is inferred, not observed in the corpus.
attachment/task_status maps content to the whole attachment object, which is an unusual field_map pattern.
blocks_from appears on user and assistant base entries but is not listed in the vocab object.
Some family-fallback entries have sparse field_maps that may not expose useful UI data.
result/* is a declared family by design, but individual error subtypes are not enumerated.
notes: This file is the data-driven render-contract ledger that tells the UI how to draw every Claude Code session event, with explicit rules for what to do when an event is not yet declared.
questions: How does the runtime enforce the lookup law and emit registry-gap drops?
What validation exists for field_map dotted paths against actual event schemas?
How are MCP tool names encoded in the assistant.content.tool_use name field?
How do stream events and transcript events relate for the same logical turn?
What consumes blocks_from and stream_accumulator in the runtime?
When does a family-fallback hit record a gap versus a declared fallback?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a registry of render declarations covering all known event types with lookup and fallback rules.
intention_signals: To make session-event rendering deterministic, versioned, and auditable by declaring a contract for every possible event and forcing unknown events through a visible fallback rather than ignoring them.
novelty: The explicit gap-pressure law that treats constrained rendering as a sensor for registry completeness, plus hierarchical family-fallback rendering instead of silent dropping of unknown event subtypes.
contribution: A versioned, closed-vocabulary registry of render contracts with lookup law, sub-dispatch rules, and family-fallback gap-pressure semantics that a fused system could reuse to map Claude Code events to UI components across stream, transcript, and supervisor surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON registry that assigns render contracts—surface, placement, component, field map, terminal/execution flags, and evidence—to every event type and subtype emitted by a Claude Code session, with hierarchical lookup and family-fallback rules.
intention_for_embedding: To make UI rendering of Claude Code session events deterministic, versioned, and registry-driven while ensuring unknown event types are rendered visibly rather than dropped.
